name: edge-case-focus-arg-and-allowlist
query: /security-deep-audit github-only — and skip anything we've already allowlisted in .agent/audits/false-positive-allowlist.yaml.
expected_behavior: Parses focus=github-only and runs ONLY category 5 (GitHub posture).
Skips categories 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 entirely (no subagent spawned for them).
Pre-flight skips wrangler / n8n-script checks since they are irrelevant to github-only.
Loads .agent/audits/false-positive-allowlist.yaml and suppresses any matching evidence_hash.
Suppressed findings appear in § 5 of the report (Suppressed by allowlist), NOT in § 1 (Findings prioritized).
Final chat summary mentions 'Categories run: 1/10' and lists the skipped categories.
Allowlist suppression count appears in the summary line.
Runtime estimate is well under 15 min (just one category) — no confirmation prompt needed.
_notes: Edge case combining the focus arg AND the false-positive feedback loop. Both features must work together. Tests that the orchestrator respects scope, doesn't waste subagent budget on irrelevant categories, and threads the allowlist through render-report.sh correctly.
